title: Singing in the Rain / बरखा मे गाएब
description:
Sukhiya Kaka was a famous singer. He met Dinoo who turned out to be his biggest fan. Together they made quite a pair!

सुखिया कका एकटा प्रसिद्ध गायक छलाह। हुनका दीनू डायनासोर सं भेंट भेलनि, जे हुनक सभसं पैघ प्रशंसक बनि गेलनि। दुनू मिलि कs कि जोड़ी बनौलनि!

pages.text:
Sukhiya Kaka lived in a village called Bitauna. 
सुखिया कका बिटौना गाम मे रहैत छलाह।  
Bitauna was famous all over Rajasthan for its singers.
बिटौना गाम पूरा राजस्थान मे अपन गवैया सब लेल प्रसिद्ध छल।

No celebration was complete without Sukhiya Kaka's sweet songs.
सुखिया ककाक मधुर गीत बिना कोनो कार्यक्रम पूर्ण नहि मानल जाइत छल।
Today he had been invited to village Jhalaura.  
आइ ओ झलौड़ा गाम से बजाओल गेल छलाह।

Sukhiya Kaka twirled his big moustache and set off.He was singing raag Malhar....Come to me o rolling clouds...
सुखिया कका अपन मोंछ पर ताव दैत बिदा भेलाह। पूरा ज़ोर लगा
कs राग मल्हार गाएब शुरू कयलनि - "आबि जा बदरवा आबि जा…"

Suddenly, he heard, dhupp dhupp dhupp dhupp!Was someone giving a beat to his song?
अनचोके ओ सुनलनि....धप धप धप धप! केओ हुनक गीत मे ताल दs रहल छल की ?

Dinoo, the dinosaur came running towards him. Sukhiya Kaka was so happy to see him!
दीनु डायनासोर हुनक गीत सुनबा लेल आबि रहल छ्ल। सुखिया कका ओकरा देखि कs प्रसन्न भs गेलाह।

You don't get such a  BIG listener every day!
Come to me o rolling clouds, shower me with lovely rain!
एतेक पैघ श्रोता सभ दिन नहि भेटैत छैक।  
"आबि जा बदरवा आबि जा ... बुन्न बरसौने जाह!"

Sukhiya Kaka and Dinoo sang a duet and did a little jig to keep time.
सुखिया कका आ दिनू, दुनू मिलि कs युगल गीत गौलनि आ ठुमका सेहो लगौलनि।

The clouds just had to listen to them! Lightning flashed and the wind danced. Dark clouds came rolling in. 
बादल कोना नहि सुनितय? बिजुरी चमकs लागल, हवा बहs लागल, आ करिया मेघ चारू दिस सँ अन्हार कs देलक।

Sukhiya Kaka and Dinoo laughed with joy. 
ई देखि दिनू आ सुखिया कका आनन्दविभोर भs गेलाह।

As the rain came pouring down, Sukhiya Kaka and Dinoo shared a small umbrella.
झमाझम बरखा होमs लागल। सुखिया कका आ दिनू छोटका छत्ता तानलनि ।

Then off they went to Jhalaura following the smell of yummy pakoras, singing
पकौड़ाक सुगंध के पछोड़ धs दुनू गोटे गीत गबैत झलौड़ा गाम दिस बिदा भेलाह।

Dear Reader,
प्रिय पाठक,
Have you ever seen a dinosaur?
की अहाँ कहियो डायनासोर देखने छी?
Do you think they would have sung songs?
की डायनासोर गीत गबैत होयतैक?
What do you like to do in the rain?
बरखा मे अहाँ कें की-की करब नीक लगैत अछि?
What do you like to eat when it rains?
बरखा मे अहाँ कें की-की खायब नीक लगैत अछि?

Acknowledgements
We wish to thank Mr. Pradip Bihari, theatre aficionado, creator of stories,
published writer, proficient in Maithili, Hindi and Nepali and recipient of the Sahitya Akademi Award, 2007, for supporting us and for graciously reviewing the translation of this book.
We wish to thank iSaksham for coordinating the first draft of this translation.

Aripana Foundation is a not-for-profit organization based in Darbhanga, that works in the areas of education, arts & culture and primary health. Its geographical scope is the Mithila region of Bihar.
Aripana Foundation's Project Lemon Choos aims to create and make available, quality children’s literature in Maithili – a language of north Bihar, foremost on many a children’s tongue, yet struggling to hold its own against other socially and politically dominant languages. In addition to creating original works in Maithili, the project also involves translations of some of the choicest works in children’s literature from across the world, that validate children’s imagination and keep their inner world thriving. We hope Maithil children savour these books, much like the Lemon Choos we all savored as children.
To know more please visit: www.aripanafoundation.org
